danger_signals.visual_form: Sudden tile transition; no forewarning in dungeon layout
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate high-difficulty combat; often results in character death

danger_signals.visual_form: Labyrinthine tile-based design with dead ends and hidden paths
danger_signals.meaning: Player disorientation; risk of sudden monster encounters; danger of becoming trapped

danger_signals.visual_form: Save functionality only available in town areas
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability between towns; resource depletion risk in dungeons
Character death or incapacitation; immediate game over if protagonist Rooks

danger_signals.visual_form: 42-item non-stacking inventory limit displayed in UI
danger_signals.meaning: Resource scarcity; inability to carry restorative items; tactical vulnerability

safety_signals.visual_form: Transition from dungeon tile-view to town area
safety_signals.meaning: Save point availability; NPC healing services; respite from combat

safety_signals.visual_form: NPC present in each town offering resurrection service
safety_signals.meaning: Fallen elemental spirits can be revived for fee; party recovery possible
Successful combat; progression toward character advancement

safety_signals.visual_form: Four distinct elemental cards (Sylph, Efrite, Dao, Marid) in party rotation
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical flexibility; ability to swap elemental types; redundancy for dead spirits

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Persecuted group hunted across Elemen; presence in towns as NPCs or mentors

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Minions and monsters encountered in dungeons under Galneon's control
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dungeons progressively controlled by Galneon; increasing monster difficulty; towns under regime authority
Dissolved faction; Rooks's father was member; historical faction referenced in narrative

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ancient evil manifestations; corrupted creatures; chaos-aligned monsters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mysterious events across land; sealed dungeons; cataclysmic environmental signs

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Card Masters active; Rimsala sealed; stable kingdoms
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs reference historical stability; Card Master lineage respected

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Civil war period (10 years prior to game start)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Kingdom conflict; armies engaged; Card Masters hunted
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs recall war trauma; death of Rooks's father; Knights of Lexford dissolution

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dungeons filled with minions; monsters increase in strength; towns under regime control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs fearful; Card Masters hunted; Rooks must hide/develop abilities; mysterious cataclysmic events occurring

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mysterious events across land; increased monster activity; environmental corruption signs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Urgent NPC dialogue; escalating danger; time pressure on player to prevent awakening

environmental_storytelling.example: Living characters represented as intact cards; death/incapacity shown as torn cards; visual representation reinforces card game theme and character fragility

environmental_storytelling.technique: Dungeon layout design as narrative hazard
environmental_storytelling.example: Labyrinthine passages with dead ends and hidden dangers reflect state of chaos in Elemen; player disorientation mirrors Rooks's uncertain journey

environmental_storytelling.technique: NPC healer presence as faction safety marker
environmental_storytelling.example: Spirit healer in each town signals Card Master infrastructure and knowledge; NPC presence indicates settled areas versus hostile dungeons
Monster difficulty increases as player advances through dungeons; reflects Galneon's growing power and approaching cataclysm

environmental_storytelling.technique: Save point restriction as world hostility
environmental_storytelling.example: Save functionality limited to towns only; reflects dangerous world outside civilization; visual isolation of safety zones

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can switch elemental spirits; cast powerful but expensive spell cards; flee battles; essential for party survival
Entire party crippled; immediate game over condition; highest stakes for player
Rotate through same party slot; occupy one of four party positions; provide elemental tactical options
Can be rotated out for living elemental; can be resurrected by spirit healer for fee; game continues (unlike human character death)
Results in immediate game over; higher stakes than elemental death
Often appears without forewarning; triggered by tile movement; high damage output; frequently causes player character death
Turn-based combat; variable damage; defeat yields experience and gold; difficulty increases in deeper dungeons

notes: Arcana communicates world-state primarily through narrative context and gameplay mechanics rather than direct visual representation. The 'card' metaphor serves dual purpose: narrative (Card Masters as faction) and mechanical (character death as torn cards). The game emphasizes danger through systematic constraints (limited saves, instant game-overs, inventory caps) rather than explicit visual warnings. Political situation (Galneon's oppression, impending Rimsala resurrection) is conveyed through NPC dialogue and quest context. Visual communication is minimal due to SNES technical constraints; meaning derives from mechanical consequences and tile-based level design. First-person perspective and labyrinthine dungeons reinforce player vulnerability and world hostility.
